prompts: Alternating current (AC) waveform is typically represented as a sinusoidal wave, indicating its periodic change in direction and magnitude over time. This sinusoidal pattern means that AC voltage oscillates above and below the zero line, typically following a sine curve. In contrast, direct current (DC) is depicted as a straight line parallel to the time axis, representing a constant direction and magnitude of flow. Thus, while AC voltage varies with time, DC voltage remains constant, highlighting a key difference in how each type of current behaves.
